meta.title: Ethika Parents AI Toolkit
meta.description: A bilingual toolkit helping parents navigate AI and children's digital lives.

home.from_sek_position: Our position on AI in academic work
home.from_sek_position_text: Ethika International School embraces AI as a learning tool while maintaining clear boundaries around academic integrity. Students are encouraged to explore AI with teacher guidance.
home.whats_new_item1: Crisis flow updated with new SDAIA reporting channels
home.whats_new_item2: New family activities added for ages 10–13

crisis.title: If Something Happened
crisis.subtitle: Choose the situation closest to yours. We'll give you clear steps.
crisis.deepfake: A fake image or video of my child
crisis.voice_scam: A voice call that may have been a scam
crisis.bullying: My child is being bullied online
crisis.chatbot: My child seems attached to an AI chatbot
crisis.inappropriate: Inappropriate content my child saw or was sent
crisis.deepfake_s1: Stay calm. Tell your child they are not in trouble and this is not their fault.
crisis.deepfake_s2: Take screenshots of the image/video with the URL visible. Do not delete the original.
crisis.deepfake_s3: Report to the platform immediately using their abuse/CSAM reporting tool.
crisis.deepfake_c1: Kollona Amn app or call 911 — choose 'cybercrimes'
crisis.deepfake_preserve: Screenshots with URLs, dates, times, usernames. Save everything before reporting for deletion.
crisis.deepfake_after: Read our guide on deepfakes and fake images for longer-term steps.
crisis.voice_s1: Do not return the call or engage further. If money was sent, contact your bank immediately.
crisis.voice_s2: Record the phone number, time of call, and what was said. Save any voicemails.
crisis.voice_s3: Report to local police (911) and the NCA cybersecurity incident portal.
crisis.voice_preserve: Phone number, call time, voicemail recordings, any transaction details.
crisis.voice_after: Read our guide on AI voice cloning scams for how to protect your family.
crisis.bully_s1: Listen to your child without judgment. Tell them you believe them and will help.
crisis.bully_s2: Document everything: screenshots of messages, profiles, dates and times.
crisis.bully_s3: Block the account(s) and report to the platform. Contact the school counsellor.
crisis.bully_c2: Kollona Amn app for serious threats
crisis.bully_preserve: All messages, screenshots, profile URLs, dates, times. Do not delete conversations.
crisis.bully_after: Read our guide on AI and bullying for ongoing support.
crisis.chatbot_s1: Do not take the device away suddenly — this can feel like losing a friend to the child.
crisis.chatbot_s2: Have an open conversation: ask your child to show you the chatbot and what they talk about.
crisis.chatbot_s3: Set a time boundary together — reduce gradually, do not cut off abruptly.
crisis.chatbot_c2: Family therapist if attachment seems deep
crisis.chatbot_preserve: Note which app/chatbot, how long they've used it, and what topics come up.
crisis.chatbot_after: Read our guide on AI chatbots and emotional attachment.
crisis.inappropriate_s1: Stay calm. Do not shame or blame your child — they need your support right now.
crisis.inappropriate_s2: Ask what they saw and how it made them feel. Listen more than you speak.
crisis.inappropriate_s3: Report the content on the platform and enable appropriate parental controls.
crisis.inappropriate_c2: Kollona Amn if content involved minors
crisis.inappropriate_preserve: Screenshots of what was seen, the platform, the sender if applicable.
crisis.inappropriate_after: Read our guide on talking to your child about inappropriate content.
crisis.other_s1: Take a moment to breathe. You're doing the right thing by seeking help.
crisis.other_s2: Write down what happened — the facts, dates, and who was involved.
crisis.other_s3: Contact the Ethika school counsellor as a first step — they can help triage.
crisis.other_c2: Kollona Amn (911) for anything that feels urgent
crisis.other_preserve: Any evidence: screenshots, messages, dates, times, usernames.
crisis.other_after: Browse our risk guides or contact the school for personalized guidance.

tryAi.title: Try AI Together
tryAi.subtitle: Age-appropriate activities you can do with your child this weekend.
tryAi.agreement_desc: A printable agreement you can fill in with your child and stick on the fridge.
tryAi.ksa_no: May not be available in KSA
tryAi.age_warning: This tool's minimum age ({min}) exceeds this age band. Consider the alternative below.
tryAi.age_6_9_dev: Children this age are developing critical thinking and can start understanding that AI is a tool made by people. They benefit from guided, hands-on exploration with a parent present. Keep sessions short (15–30 minutes) and focused on creation, not consumption.
tryAi.age_10_13_dev: Pre-teens are curious and increasingly independent online. They can understand concepts like bias, privacy, and how AI makes predictions. This is the ideal age to build critical AI literacy — the ability to question what AI produces rather than accept it at face value.

risks.title: Understand the Risks
risks.subtitle: Read at your own pace. Each topic includes what to watch for and what to do.
risks.content_warning_text: This page describes real incidents including some involving minors. You can read this in your own time and skip any section.
risks.incidents_title: What it looks like in practice
risks.what_to_do: What to do if it happens
risks.talk_title: How to talk to your child before it happens
risks.deepfakes_summary: Deepfake technology uses AI to create realistic-looking fake images, videos, or audio of real people. Anyone's photo — including your child's — can be used without consent to create fake content that looks genuine. This technology is becoming easier and cheaper to use.
risks.deepfakes_incident1: In 2024, AI-generated explicit images of Taylor Swift went viral on social media, reaching tens of millions of views before platforms removed them. The same technology is used against ordinary people, including minors.
risks.deepfakes_incident2: A mother in Spain discovered AI-generated fake images of her teenage daughter being shared in school group chats. The images were created from normal social media photos.
risks.deepfakes_sign1: Sudden withdrawal from social media or refusing to take photos
risks.deepfakes_sign2: Anxiety about images of themselves being online
risks.deepfakes_sign3: Being teased or bullied with references to photos or videos
risks.deepfakes_sign4: Secretive behavior around their phone or laptop
risks.deepfakes_talk_young: Ages 6–9: 'Some people use computers to make pretend pictures that look real. If anyone ever shows you a strange picture of you or a friend, come tell me right away. You won't be in trouble.'
risks.deepfakes_talk_older: Ages 10–16: 'There's technology called deepfakes that can put someone's face on a different body or make fake videos. It's being used to hurt people. Let's talk about what to do if you ever see something like this — or if it happens to you or someone you know.'
risks.voice_cloning_summary: AI voice cloning can replicate someone's voice from just a few seconds of audio — a social media video, a voice message, or even a phone greeting. Scammers use this to impersonate family members, usually claiming an emergency and asking for money.
risks.voice_cloning_incident1: In Arizona, a mother received a phone call from what sounded exactly like her 15-year-old daughter crying and saying she'd been kidnapped. It was an AI-cloned voice. The scammers demanded ransom.
risks.voice_cloning_incident2: A British company transferred $25 million after an employee received a video call from what appeared to be their CFO — the face and voice were both AI-generated deepfakes.
risks.voice_cloning_sign1: Unexpected urgent calls from a family member's number
risks.voice_cloning_sign2: Caller demands immediate action and tells you not to hang up
risks.voice_cloning_sign3: Something sounds 'off' even though the voice is familiar
risks.voice_cloning_sign4: Request for money transfer, gift cards, or cryptocurrency
risks.voice_cloning_talk_young: Ages 6–9: 'Sometimes bad people use tricks on the phone to sound like someone you know. If someone calls and says something scary, hang up and tell me.'
risks.voice_cloning_talk_older: Ages 10–16: 'AI can now copy anyone's voice from a short recording. Scammers use this to pretend to be family members in trouble. Our family code word is [choose one]. If you ever get a scary call, ask for the code word.'

rights.title: Know Your Rights
rights.subtitle: Saudi Arabia has strong digital protection laws. Here's what they mean for your family.
rights.sdaia_desc: The Saudi Data & AI Authority (SDAIA) governs AI development and use in the Kingdom. They have established seven AI ethics principles.
rights.sdaia_p2: Transparency — AI decisions must be explainable
rights.sdaia_p3: Security — AI systems must be secure
rights.sdaia_p4: Privacy — Personal data must be protected
rights.sdaia_p5: Accountability — Clear responsibility for AI outcomes
rights.sdaia_p6: Social benefit — AI must serve society
rights.sdaia_p7: Reliability — AI must work as intended
rights.sdaia_complaint_link: Visit the SDAIA complaints portal to report AI ethics violations.
rights.pdpl_desc: The PDPL gives you specific rights over your and your children's personal data.
rights.pdpl_right1: Right to know what data is collected about your child
rights.pdpl_right2: Right to request correction of inaccurate data
rights.pdpl_right3: Right to request deletion of your child's data
rights.pdpl_right4: Right to withdraw consent for data processing
rights.pdpl_right5: Right to file a complaint about data breaches
rights.pdpl_how_delete_text: Contact the company or platform directly with a written request citing the PDPL. If they don't respond within 30 days, file a complaint with SDAIA.
rights.cybercrime_desc: Saudi Arabia's Anti-Cybercrime Law criminalizes online harassment, unauthorized access to data, and creation of defamatory digital content.
rights.cybercrime_item1: Cyberbullying and online harassment — up to 1 year imprisonment and 500,000 SAR fine
rights.cybercrime_item2: Creating or distributing defamatory content (including deepfakes) — up to 1 year and 500,000 SAR
rights.cybercrime_item3: Unauthorized access to personal data — up to 4 years and 3,000,000 SAR
rights.cybercrime_item4: Exploiting minors online — severe penalties including extended imprisonment
rights.nca_desc: The NCA is responsible for cybersecurity across the Kingdom. They handle cybersecurity incidents and provide guidance on staying safe online.
rights.nca_report: Report a cybersecurity incident to the NCA through their portal.
rights.child_protection_desc: Championed by the Crown Prince, this initiative led to a landmark UN resolution on children's rights in cyberspace. Saudi Arabia is a global leader in digital child protection.
rights.child_protection_un: In 2024, the UN General Assembly adopted the Saudi-led resolution on protecting children in cyberspace — the first of its kind, co-sponsored by over 60 countries.
rights.reporting_desc: One page, one tap each. Know where to go when you need to act.
rights.reporting_kollona_desc: Saudi Arabia's official safety reporting app. Report cybercrimes, abuse, or any safety concern.
rights.reporting_911_desc: For immediate threats to safety. Ask for the cybercrime unit if relevant.
rights.reporting_sdaia_portal_desc: Report AI ethics violations and data protection breaches.
rights.reporting_nca_portal_desc: Report cybersecurity incidents including hacking and data theft.
rights.reporting_school_desc: Your first point of contact for school-related digital safety concerns.
rights.act_now: If you need to act on this, here's how

readDeeper.title: Read Deeper
readDeeper.subtitle: Curated by Ethika's team — not exhaustive, but trusted. Each recommendation includes why we chose it.
readDeeper.islamic_desc: A companion resource authored in partnership with respected Saudi Islamic educators. For parents who want guidance through an Islamic lens.
readDeeper.islamic_note: This is a perspective for parents who want it — not required reading. It connects Islamic values of trust (amana), protection of children, and wisdom (hikma) to practical AI decisions.

fromSek.title: From Ethika
fromSek.subtitle: Signed statements from Ethika leadership. Not marketing — accountability.
fromSek.academic_title: Our Position on AI in Academic Work
fromSek.academic_content:
Ethika International School recognizes AI as a transformative educational tool. Our position:

• AI tools may be used for research assistance, brainstorming, and learning support when explicitly permitted by the teacher.
• Students must disclose AI use in all submitted work.
• Submitting AI-generated text as original work is an academic integrity violation.
• Teachers will provide clear guidance on acceptable AI use for each assignment.
• We assess students on their thinking process, not just final output.

We review this position each semester as the technology evolves.
fromSek.academic_signer: Signed: Dr. Sarah Al-Rashid, Head of Academic Affairs
fromSek.classroom_title: AI in the Classroom at Ethika
fromSek.classroom_content:
Currently in use at Ethika:

• Khanmigo (Khan Academy) — supervised, for math and science tutoring (Grades 4+)
• Google Workspace AI features — supervised, for writing feedback (Grades 6+)
• Scratch and AI4K12 — for understanding how AI works (all grades)

Not permitted:
• ChatGPT, Claude, or similar general-purpose LLMs used unsupervised
• Any AI tool that requires a student to create a personal account without parental consent
• AI tools for assessment or grading of students
fromSek.classroom_signer: Signed: Mr. James Chen, Director of Learning Technology
fromSek.escalation_title: If You Want to Raise a Concern
fromSek.escalation_content:
The in-school escalation path:

1. Start with your child's teacher — they know the classroom context.
2. School Counsellor — for wellbeing concerns or if the teacher can't resolve it.
3. Head of School — for policy-level concerns or if previous steps are unsatisfactory.
4. External reporting — Kollona Amn or SDAIA if it involves a legal matter.

You can reach anyone in this chain directly. You never need permission to escalate.
fromSek.escalation_signer: Signed: Ms. Noura Al-Faisal, Head of School
fromSek.workshop_desc: Stay connected with our parent community.
fromSek.workshop_date: May 15, 2026 — 'AI Safety: What Every Parent Needs to Know'
fromSek.parents_asked_desc: Themes from our last workshop, with our commitments.
fromSek.parents_q1: Will you ban AI tools entirely?
fromSek.parents_a1: No. We believe guided AI use builds better digital citizens than prohibition. But we will always prioritize safety over convenience.
fromSek.parents_q2: How will you handle deepfakes of students?
fromSek.parents_a2: Any deepfake involving a student triggers immediate investigation, platform reporting, and family support. We treat this as seriously as physical safety threats.
